title: Projects
text:
COVID-19 US Interactive Dashboard
    An interactive Shiny dashboard visualizing COVID-19 cases, deaths, and vaccination rates across all 50 US states from 2020-2023 with spatial mapping.
  

  
    
    A Machine Learning Approach to Identifying Malaria Risk Factors Among Pregnant Women in Ghana
    Applied Random Forest, Decision Tree, Naïve Bayes, and KNN to identify malaria risk factors among pregnant women using the 2019 Ghana Malaria Indicator Survey.

title: Teaching
text:
University of Minnesota Duluth, Department of Mathematics and Statistics
Teaching Assistant
Fall 2025
Spring 2026
A two-semester sequence covering limits, derivatives, and applications of differentiation in Calculus I, followed by integration techniques, sequences, series, and applications in Calculus II.

title: Teaching
text:
Differential Equation





University of Minnesota Duluth, Department of Mathematics and Statistics
Teaching Assistant
Fall 2025
Spring 2026
An undergraduate course covering first and second order ordinary differential equations, systems of differential equations, and series solutions with applications to mathematical modeling using Mathematica and Python.

title: Teaching
text:
Statistical Inference





University of Ghana, Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science
Teaching Assistant
Summer 2023
Spring 2024
Covers fundamental principles of statistical inference including point estimation, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and Bayesian methods.

title: Teaching
text:
Introduction to Biostatistics





University of Ghana, Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science
Teaching Assistant
Spring 2024
An introductory course covering fundamental concepts in biostatistics including study design, descriptive statistics, probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and survival analysis applied to public health and medical research.

title: Teaching
text:
Advanced Linear Regression





University of Ghana, Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science
Teaching Assistant
Summer 2023
An advanced course covering simple and multiple linear regression, model building strategies, variable selection, multicollinearity, residual diagnostics, and remedial measures with applications using real datasets.

title: Teaching
text:
Decision Theory





University of Ghana, Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science
Teaching Assistant
Spring 2024
A course exploring the mathematical foundations of decision making under uncertainty, covering game theory, risk analysis, and statistical decision rules including Bayes and minimax criteria.

title: Awards & Honors
text:
Summer Research Funding (GAGE) - University of Minnesota Duluth Awarded competitive summer research funding through the Graduate Assistantship in Graduate Education (GAGE) program to support thesis research in statistics at the University of Minnesota Duluth.
Fostering Diversity in Biostatistics Workshop Grant - University of Minnesota Duluth Received a competitive diversity grant to attend the Fostering Diversity in Biostatistics Workshop at the ENAR Conference, supporting underrepresented scholars in biostatistics and statistical sciences.

title: Awards & Honors
text:
2023

First Place — Mathematics Society Quiz Competition - University of Ghana Awarded first place in the annual Mathematics Society Quiz Competition at the University of Ghana, demonstrating excellence in mathematical reasoning and problem-solving.
First Place — Statistics and Actuarial Science Quiz Competition - University of Ghana Awarded first place in the Statistics and Actuarial Science Quiz Competition, demonstrating outstanding knowledge in statistical theory and actuarial methods.

title: Awards & Honors
text:
2022

Honor of Excellence and Dedication — University of Ghana Actuarial and Statistics Society
Recognized for two years of transformative leadership as Academic Committee member. —

title: Bernard Kwabena Appiah
text:
I am a graduate statistician with experience in spatial analysis, survival models, and systematic reviews. I am currently pursuing an M.S. in Statistics at the University of Minnesota Duluth, and I hold a B.Sc. in Mathematical Science from the University of Ghana.
My work focuses on applying statistical methods to public health challenges, with interests in applied methodology, population health, and data-informed policy. I value clear communication, rigorous analysis, and collaborative problem-solving.
